content.scenario: A client is furious because their service has been down for 2 hours, hurting their business. They demand a full refund and are shouting.
options: Interrupt and state the Terms of Service which limit outage liabilities.
Empathize with their loss, explain that the engineering team is actively mitigating, and offer a proactive service credit.
Escalate the ticket to your manager immediately without engaging them.
content.consequences: Option 1 causes immediate escalation and churn. Option 3 frustrates the client because of transfer delays. Option 2 successfully validates their problem, diffuses anger, and provides a clear next step.
content.success_metric: Customer retention rate = 100% and customer satisfaction score (CSAT) >= 4/5
content.spanish: Manejo de incidentes con clientes enojados debido a interrupciones críticas en sistemas de producción.

content.scenario: A Product Manager requests an out-of-scope feature 3 days before code freeze, claiming it is vital for a customer demo.
options: Agree to build it by working overtime, risking code stability.
Refuse immediately, causing friction with the product team.
Explain the risk to launch stability and propose scheduling it for Phase 2 or swapping it with a lower-priority feature.
content.consequences: Option 1 leads to bugs in production and team burn-out. Option 2 damages cross-team relationship. Option 3 negotiates scope constructively while protecting the release quality.
content.success_metric: On-time release rate = 100% and zero critical bugs introduced in production
content.spanish: Negociación de alcance (scope creep) de último momento con product managers salvaguardando la calidad del código.

content.scenario: A Senior Manager asks you to bypass standard pull request security gates to deploy a quick bug fix directly to main.
options: Bypass the gates to maintain manager approval.
Politely decline, explain the compliance risk, and offer to expedite a fast-track code review through normal gates.
Report the manager to compliance immediately.
content.consequences: Option 1 compromises audit logs and introduces risk. Option 3 causes severe professional damage. Option 2 upholds compliance rules while offering a practical alternative.
content.success_metric: Compliance audit compliance = 100% and time to hotfix deploy under 1 hour
content.spanish: Rechazar peticiones para saltarse los protocolos de seguridad de código de forma constructiva.

content.scenario: You realize a database migration task is taking longer than planned, and you will miss the Friday release deadline.
options: Hope to finish it late and deploy on Saturday without notifying anyone.
Alert the team immediately on Wednesday, explain the blockers, and present a revised estimate with testing coverage details.
Wait until Friday afternoon to notify stakeholders of the delay.
content.consequences: Option 1 causes stealth deployments which lead to outages. Option 3 leaves no time for the team to pivot. Option 2 allows the team to adjust schedules and assist in unblocking.
content.success_metric: Team coordination rating = High and stakeholder surprise rate = 0%
content.spanish: Notificación anticipada de retrasos en entregas críticas de ingeniería.

content.scenario: A peer left comments blocking your PR, insisting on an architectural pattern you believe is over-engineered.
options: Argue in the PR comments section until they back down.
Ignore the comments, ask another peer for approval, and merge.
Schedule a quick 10-minute sync to understand their concerns, present your trade-offs, and align on a compromise.
content.consequences: Option 1 causes toxic comment threads. Option 2 bypasses peer trust. Option 3 fosters collaborative discussion and prevents miscommunication.
content.success_metric: Pull Request review cycle time under 24 hours and team trust index = High
content.spanish: Resolución de desacuerdos arquitectónicos bloqueantes durante la revisión de código.
